title: BEM-915 curator response
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL_RESPONSE.md with content # BEM-915 TELEGRAM OPERATOR INTERFACE PROTOCOL RESPONSE
Status: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Worker approves adding Telegram Operator Interface to the system improvement protocol.
- Current Telegram behavior is insufficient: passive hourly no-change reports do not solve operator tasking and report delivery.
- Telegram must become the primary operator-facing control channel for routine task submission, status queries, gate decisions and role-based reports.
Evidence:
- Operator screenshot showing hourly reports with no changes and no working task interaction.
- Deno healthCheck confirms Telegram webhook service exists.
- governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM915_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_PROTOCOL_REQUEST.md
- governance/audit/internal/results/BEM915_WORKER_TELEGRAM_OPERATOR_INTERFACE_REVIEW.md
Agreed changes:
- Update the BEM-914 improvement protocol with a Telegram Operator Interface layer.
- Add commands: /status, /task, /approve, /reject, /gate, /report, /help.
- Add structured Telegram task envelope with trace_id, task_id, source, target object, priority, policy decision and proof path.
- Add routing to Curator, Director and General Director.
- Add reporting levels: Curator operational report, Director planning/rules report, General Director strategic gate report.
- Add delivery receipts, correlation IDs, error states, E2E selftest and acceptance criteria.
Operator gates:
- real bot token/secrets, production webhook changes, issue #31 writes, schedule triggers and always-on daemon model.
Recommendation:
- Submit updated protocol to operator for review.
No issue comments.
